title: The Living Loop
subtitle: How Floor 318 feeds thousands from fish, plants, and compost
intro: Beneath Mel's hands-in-the-dirt intuition runs a closed-loop aquaponics system — three nested cycles operating at different time scales, monitored by AI, feeding an entire floor of Arcology One.

timeScales.label: Minutes
timeScales.description: The water cycle: fish excrete ammonia, bacteria convert it to nitrate, water recirculates through grow beds and back.

timeScales.label: Weeks
timeScales.description: The growth cycle: plants uptake nutrients over 30-35 days. Lettuce, herbs, tomatoes — each on their own schedule, optimized by light and feeding rate.

timeScales.label: Months
timeScales.description: The waste cycle: fish sludge is separated, composted or digested, and nutrients are returned to the water. Biogas offsets energy costs.

nodes.label: Fish Tank
nodes.description: Tilapia at 80 fish/m³ produce ammonia as their primary waste product. Less than 30% of nitrogen in fish feed is assimilated into fish bodies — the rest drives the entire nutrient cycle.
nodes.aiRole: AI classifies fish health state (Healthy / Stress / Mortality) from pH, dissolved oxygen, and temperature data with 99.3% accuracy using Random Forest models.
nodes.storyQuote: Mel's hands were wrist-deep in the planter bed, feeling the quality of the soil between their thumb and forefinger.

nodes.label: Solid Filter
nodes.description: Separates fish sludge from water. The sludge contains 4.47% nitrogen and 2.35% phosphorus — too valuable to discard. Most systems waste this; Arcology One doesn't.

nodes.label: Biofilter
nodes.description: Nitrifying bacteria perform a two-step conversion: Nitrosomonas oxidize ammonia to nitrite, then Nitrospira oxidize nitrite to nitrate — the form plants can absorb. Recently discovered comammox Nitrospira can do both steps alone.
nodes.aiRole: AI monitors total ammonia nitrogen (TAN) levels. Safe threshold: <1 mg/L. Functional limit: <3.2 mg/L. Above that, fish stress cascades through the entire system.

nodes.label: Grow Beds
nodes.description: Plants uptake nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and trace minerals from the nitrate-rich water. Optimal ratio: 60g of fish feed per day per m² of growing area. At this ratio, lettuce yields reach 14.51 kg/m².
nodes.storyQuote: Every corridor, public space, and residential unit on 318 was overgrown with vegetation.

nodes.label: Compost Digester
nodes.description: Anaerobic digestion breaks down fish sludge and plant waste, recovering 76% of nitrogen and 80% of phosphorus. The process also produces biogas, partially offsetting the system's energy needs.

nodes.label: LED Grow Lights
nodes.description: LED arrays provide 50,000–70,000 lux for photosynthesis. Lighting is 60–70% of total energy use in vertical farms — the single biggest lever for AI optimization.
nodes.aiRole: AI optimizes LED spectrum, intensity, and scheduling per growth stage. Research shows 82.1% energy reduction through intelligent light management alone.

nodes.label: AI Monitoring
nodes.description: Sensors throughout the system feed real-time data to ML models running on edge hardware (Raspberry Pi / ESP32). Eight parameters monitored continuously. The AI sees what Mel feels.
nodes.aiRole: Monitors pH, dissolved oxygen, TAN, electrical conductivity, water temperature, light intensity, humidity, and feed rate. Predicts yield with >95% accuracy. Fish health classification at 99.3%.
nodes.storyQuote: They touched every plant within reach. A pinch of basil rolled and brought to the nose, a thumbnail drawn across a tomato stem. Touch, check, move on.

metrics.label: Water Savings
metrics.comparison: vs. traditional farming (3 L/kg vs 250 L/kg lettuce)

metrics.label: pH Sweet Spot
metrics.comparison: bacteriological optimum; too low kills nitrifiers, too high precipitates phosphorus

metrics.label: Safe Ammonia
metrics.comparison: functional limit 3.2 mg/L before fish stress
